api: Churnkey Data API + Event Tracking API (https://api.churnkey.co)
scope: Pass B full-surface expansion: every documented Churnkey Data API read endpoint and every documented Event Tracking API single-record write endpoint is covered. Bulk-batch write variants (accepting an array body rather than one object per call) are excluded as duplicate_of their single-record counterparts, since the engine's declarative write dialect issues exactly one request per record with a JSON-object body (no array-body write primitive). GDPR data-subject-request endpoints are excluded (dsr/access as non_data_endpoint — a compliance workflow trigger, not reverse-ETL row data; dsr/delete as destructive_admin). The customer-data-endpoint webhook Churnkey calls INTO the customer's own backend during a hosted cancel-flow session is not a Churnkey API surface at all (Churnkey is the caller, not us) and is out of scope by construction.

excluded.reason: bulk variant of POST /v1/api/events/new; accepts an array of up to 100 event objects in one request body, but the engine's write dialect issues one JSON-object-body request per record — create_event already covers the identical per-event shape one record at a time

bulk variant of POST /v1/api/events/customer-update/set-users; accepts an array of customer objects in one request body, no per-record equivalent in the engine's one-request-per-record write dialect — set_billing_users already covers the identical per-customer shape
triggers a GDPR data-subject access request workflow (an async compliance process, not a synchronous reverse-ETL row write or read); not a business-data resource
GDPR customer-data deletion; irreversible admin-level mutation out of scope for a reverse-ETL connector

this is a webhook Churnkey itself calls INTO the customer's own backend during a hosted cancel-flow session to fetch extra customer attributes — Churnkey is the HTTP client here, not this connector; there is no Churnkey-hosted endpoint to read or write
